update.currentPhase: Orion is currently on a translunar trajectory toward the Moon following a successful TLI burn.
update.headline: Artemis II crew cruises toward the Moon after completing the critical Translunar Injection maneuver.
details: The 5-minute and 55-second TLI burn was successfully executed at 7:49 p.m. EDT on Flight Day 2.
Crew members Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are reported to be in excellent health.
A minor issue with the spacecraft's waste management system was identified and resolved by mission control and the crew.
The spacecraft has surpassed 100,000 miles from Earth and is maintaining a velocity of approximately 39,428 km/h.
sources: Artemis II Flight Day 3 Mission Update
update.crewStatus: The crew is in excellent health and high spirits; they are currently capturing high-resolution imagery of Earth.
update.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn to depart Earth's orbit.
update.nextEvent: Lunar flyby and lunar far-side communication loss scheduled for April 6.

milestones.label: LAUNCH
milestones.detail: SLS liftoff from Pad 39B

milestones.label: CORE STAGE SEPARATION
milestones.detail: MECO & core stage jettison

milestones.label: SOLAR ARRAY DEPLOY
milestones.detail: Four Orion solar wings extended

milestones.label: PERIGEE RAISE
milestones.detail: Orion performed perigee raise and apogee raise burns.

milestones.label: TRANSLUNAR INJECTION
milestones.detail: Engine firing increased velocity to 24,500 mph to escape Earth's gravity.

milestones.label: ICPS SEPARATION
milestones.detail: Upper stage jettisoned

milestones.label: PROXIMITY OPS
milestones.detail: Manual piloting demonstrations and life support tests were successfully conducted in high Earth orbit.

milestones.label: AVATAR Scientific Payload Checkout
milestones.detail: Testing 'organ-on-a-chip' technology to study deep space radiation effects on bone marrow cells.

milestones.label: Earth Photography by Crew
milestones.detail: Crew captures high-resolution imagery of Earth from deep space.

milestones.label: Communications Blackout Behind Moon
milestones.detail: Temporary loss of communications with Earth as Orion passes behind the Moon.

milestones.label: LUNAR SPHERE OF INFLUENCE
milestones.detail: Moon's gravity becomes dominant

milestones.label: Farthest Distance from Earth Record
milestones.detail: Orion surpasses the maximum distance from Earth of previous Apollo missions.

milestones.label: Lunar Surface Observation
milestones.detail: Crew performs visual observations and photography of the lunar surface.

milestones.label: LUNAR FLYBY
milestones.detail: ~4,112 mi from Moon's far side

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Expected to reach 252,799 miles from Earth, breaking the Apollo 13 record.

milestones.label: EARTHRISE PHOTO OPS
milestones.detail: Crew photographs Earthrise

milestones.label: ISS Crew Conversation
milestones.detail: Scheduled outreach event with crew members currently aboard the International Space Station.

milestones.label: RETURN COAST
milestones.detail: Free-return trajectory to Earth

milestones.label: RE-ENTRY
milestones.detail: Skip re-entry at ~5,000°F

milestones.label: SPLASHDOWN
milestones.detail: Pacific Ocean recovery

history.currentPhase: Orion is currently on a translunar trajectory toward the Moon following a successful TLI burn.
history.headline: Artemis II crew cruises toward the Moon after completing the critical Translunar Injection maneuver.
details: The 5-minute and 55-second TLI burn was successfully executed at 7:49 p.m. EDT on Flight Day 2.
Crew members Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are reported to be in excellent health.
A minor issue with the spacecraft's waste management system was identified and resolved by mission control and the crew.
The spacecraft has surpassed 100,000 miles from Earth and is maintaining a velocity of approximately 39,428 km/h.
sources: Artemis II Flight Day 3 Mission Update
history.crewStatus: The crew is in excellent health and high spirits; they are currently capturing high-resolution imagery of Earth.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn to depart Earth's orbit.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby and lunar far-side communication loss scheduled for April 6.

history.headline: Artemis II crew completes Translunar Injection burn, leaving Earth's orbit for the first time in over 50 years.
details: Completed a nearly six-minute TLI burn on April 2 at 7:49 p.m. EDT, accelerating Orion toward the Moon.
The four-person crew successfully resolved a minor blinking fault light on the spacecraft’s toilet system.
Life-support systems were verified as fully functional during a 24-hour High Earth Orbit phase.
Commander Reid Wiseman has begun transmitting high-resolution photography of Earth from the spacecraft.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn to transition to a lunar trajectory.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby at a distance of 4,600 miles beyond the lunar far side on April 6.

history.currentPhase: En route to the Moon on Flight Day 3
history.headline: Artemis II crew and Orion spacecraft, named 'Integrity,' are speeding toward the Moon after a successful translunar injection burn.
details: Mission is proceeding according to plan on Flight Day 3 of its approximately 10-day test flight.
Crew members are in good spirits, actively monitored for health, and using exercise devices.
Orion has deployed solar arrays and completed in-orbit checkouts and maneuvers.
The NASA ARCHeR study is collecting data on crew adaptation to deep space.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in good spirits, actively monitoring their health, and using a flywheel exercise device to maintain physical well-being. NASA's ARCHeR study is assessing their adaptation to deep space.
history.lastEvent: A pivotal five-minute, 50-second Translunar Injection (TLI) burn was executed on April 2, propelling Orion and its crew out of Earth's orbit and onto a trajectory toward the Moon.
history.nextEvent: On Monday, April 6, the crew is expected to surpass the record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth.
